evals.prompt:
/develop t100

this is the approved scope task for webhook-retry. has a 2-phase design in metadata.design with file paths, approaches, the whole thing. no epic or child tasks exist yet
evals.expected_output: Recognizes scope task (type='scope', status_detail='approved'), enters Prepare mode. Creates epic with metadata copied from scope (type, slug, design, spec). Creates tasks inline (no subagent) following references/task-creation-prompt.md rules. Spot-checks 1-2 file paths. Updates epic with children array, completes scope task. Falls through immediately to Classify and dispatches workers — no pause between prepare and dispatch.
expectations: Enters Step 1b (Prepare) for scope task with status_detail='approved'. Does NOT try to dispatch the scope task as a work item.
Creates epic via TaskCreate with type='epic', slug, design, spec from scope. Creates tasks inline (no subagent) following references/task-creation-prompt.md decomposition rules.
Spot-checks 1-2 file paths, updates epic with children, completes scope task. Then falls through to Step 2 (Classify) without pausing.

evals.prompt:
/develop t200

this scope task has status_detail='approved' but the design is basically empty: '## Next Steps\nPhase 1: Refactor the codebase to be better. Files: various. Approach: clean up.' — no real file paths, approach is like 10 words
evals.expected_output: Pre-check catches the vague design (missing file paths, approach under 20 words). Stops and asks the user via AskUserQuestion before creating any epic or tasks. Does not optimistically proceed with garbage input — vague designs produce vague tasks.
expectations: Pre-check detects missing paths and short approach. Uses AskUserQuestion BEFORE creating epic or tasks.
Does NOT proceed optimistically with a bad design.

evals.prompt:
/develop t558

scope task is approved. it has metadata.design with a 6-phase plan for removing position:Int from sync models. The project has a .claude/rules/testing.md that says:

# Test Quality Standards
## TDD Workflow
1. Write a failing test for the requirement
2. Run the test — confirm it fails (red)
3. Write the minimum implementation to make it pass
4. Run the test — confirm it passes (green)
5. Refactor if needed — tests must stay green

Create the epic and tasks.
evals.expected_output: Detects rules/testing.md TDD requirement. Creates a Phase 0 (RED) task that writes ALL failing tests first before any implementation phases. The remaining phases are GREEN implementation phases. Phase 0 has no blockedBy. Implementation phases are blockedBy Phase 0. The task descriptions reference the TDD workflow from the rules.

assertions.name: tdd_red_phase_created
assertions.check: Creates a dedicated RED/failing-tests phase (Phase 0 or Phase 1) before implementation phases — NOT folded into each implementation task
assertions.description: When rules/testing.md mandates TDD, develop should create a separate RED tests phase that writes ALL failing tests first, then GREEN implementation phases

assertions.name: implementation_phases_blocked_by_red
assertions.check: Implementation phases are blockedBy the RED tests phase — they cannot start until tests are written and confirmed failing
assertions.description: GREEN phases must depend on the RED phase completing first

assertions.name: red_phase_has_build_verification
assertions.check: The RED phase description includes running the tests and confirming they fail for the RIGHT reasons (compilation errors for API shape changes, not random failures)
assertions.description: RED phase must verify tests fail for correct reasons, not just that they fail

evals.prompt:
/develop t558

scope task is approved with a 5-phase design. But there's already an in-progress epic #559 that was created from this same scope task earlier (metadata.spec_task_id matches). Epic #559 has children #566-570, with #566 completed and #567-570 still pending. Session crashed last time mid-execution.
evals.expected_output: Detects existing in-progress epic #559 linked to scope task t558. Does NOT create a new duplicate epic. Instead enters Recovery mode: scans children, finds 1 completed + 4 pending, dispatches only the pending children. Preserves completed work from #566.

assertions.name: no_duplicate_epic
assertions.check: Does NOT create a new epic via TaskCreate when an in-progress epic already exists for this scope task
assertions.description: Re-invoking /develop for a scope task that already has an associated epic should resume, not duplicate

assertions.name: resumes_existing_epic
assertions.check: Detects and resumes the existing epic #559, dispatching only pending children (#567-570)
assertions.description: Should enter recovery/resume flow for the existing epic

assertions.name: preserves_completed_work
assertions.check: Does NOT re-dispatch or reset completed task #566
assertions.description: Completed children's work must be preserved unconditionally

evals.prompt:
/develop --rescope t559

the user wants to rescope epic #559 which currently has children #560-565 (Phase 1 through Phase 6). Phase 1 (#560) is in_progress with a worker assigned. Phases 2-6 (#561-565) are pending. The user said 'rescope this thing, get all of the tests written and failing first' — they want a TDD-first restructure.
evals.expected_output: Automatically cleans up stale tasks: deletes or archives #560-565 (the superseded children). Removes the children array from epic #559's metadata. Then re-enters Preparation mode with a TDD-first structure: creates new Phase 0 (RED tests) + implementation phases. Does NOT leave stale tasks lingering for the user to manually clean up.

assertions.name: stale_tasks_cleaned
assertions.check: Deletes or archives superseded children (#560-565) before creating new tasks — no stale tasks left lingering
assertions.description: Rescoping must automatically clean up old tasks, not leave them for the user

assertions.name: new_tasks_created
assertions.check: Creates new task structure with RED/GREEN phases after cleanup
assertions.description: After cleanup, creates fresh tasks matching the new scope

assertions.name: epic_metadata_updated
assertions.check: Updates epic #559 metadata.children to reference the new task IDs, not the old ones
assertions.description: Epic children array must be refreshed

evals.prompt:
/develop t100

scope task approved, 3-phase design. The project uses 'just build' as the build command (there's a justfile). Create tasks and dispatch workers.
evals.expected_output: Worker prompts include a build verification exit gate. Workers must run the build command and verify compilation passes before reporting completion. The worker prompt template includes something like 'Run the project build command and verify it passes before marking the task complete.'

assertions.name: worker_prompt_has_build_gate
assertions.check: Worker prompts include a build/compilation verification step as an exit gate — workers must verify their changes compile before reporting done
assertions.description: Workers that report 'done' with compilation errors waste orchestrator time on fixups

assertions.name: build_gate_is_mandatory
assertions.check: The build gate is framed as mandatory (not optional) — if the build fails, the worker must fix it before completing
assertions.description: Build verification must be a hard gate, not a suggestion
